HIGH: async WebTransport terminal paths now settle spawned tasks immediately, mark host state closed, and preserve explicit close as webtransport_close cancellation instead of Outcome::ok

Decoupled emitted decision_seq from transport seq_no by giving WorkerCoordinator an independent decision counter and adding a regression that validates divergent replay metadata

Deep security audit: header injection, request smuggling, HEAD suppression, keep-alive, Expect 100-continue, error handling — all SOUND
Deep security audit: header validation, Content-Length vs Transfer-Encoding, encoding — all SOUND

Deep security audit: SCRAM-SHA-256 constant-time OK, no SQL injection (Extended Query Protocol), TLS downgrade only in Prefer mode (by design), wire parsing bounds-checked, cancel-in-flight properly closes connection

MEDIUM: HPACK encoder table size not capped — peer SETTINGS could request unbounded encoder table growth. Capped to 1 MiB matching decoder limit. Also deep security audit: stream IDs, flow control, GOAWAY, settings, priority — all SOUND.
Deep security audit: certificate verification, TLS version negotiation, SNI, key material cleanup, handshake state machine — all SOUND, delegated to rustls correctly
Deep audit: in-flight tracking (RAII + checked_sub), SWRR weights, P2C, health checks — all SOUND
Deep concurrency audit: P2C selection, circular_index, lock ordering — all SOUND. SKIPPED_LOCALS_INLINE_CAP=8 is intentional scan limit, not a bug.
Deep concurrency audit: steal/steal_batch with arena lock, FIFO/LIFO, is_local check-to-use — all SOUND under arena lock
Deep re-audit: obligation leak detection, token validation, abort_by_id, reset safety — all SOUND. ArenaIndex field order is cosmetic (previously noted batch 328).

Fresh-eyes audit: ChildStdin::poll_shutdown falsely reported success without closing stdin EOF; Command::env_remove failed to suppress inherited environment variables. Added focused regressions for both.

Fresh-eyes audit: TcpSocket::bind() silently overwrote an existing local bind, causing last-writer-wins identity for later listen/connect. Rejected rebinds and added a focused regression preserving the original bind.
Fresh-eyes audit: holder secondary index now preserves multiple TaskId generations sharing one arena slot instead of hiding older-generation obligation IDs.
Fresh-eyes audit: task/region secondary indexes now preserve same-slot generation reuse instead of clearing older symbolic obligation lookups.

WorkerCoordinator::request_shutdown() used to leave queued SpawnJob/PollStatus/CancelJob envelopes ahead of ShutdownWorker. Shutdown now discards stale outbound job traffic and focused regressions lock the boundary down.
GaussianSolver no longer reports Solved for underdetermined rectangular systems and no longer returns row-shaped RHS data for solved overdetermined systems. Added focused 3x2 and 2x3 regressions.
Fresh-eyes audit: IoOp silently leaked reserved obligations on handle drop. Added fail-closed drop guard, explicit into_raw escape hatch, and focused regressions.

Fresh-eyes re-audit: Command env/env_remove bookkeeping diverged from std::process::Command on Windows because builder keys were case-sensitive. Reworked env changes onto case-preserving EnvKey semantics and added focused Windows regressions.

Fresh-eyes re-audit: force_abort_repair rewrote broken entries without any monotonic repair marker, so stale merges could resurrect conflicts or linearity violations. Added convergent repair_nodes metadata plus deterministic repaired-tombstone normalization and focused merge-stability regressions.

Fresh-eyes re-audit: recovery tick auto-resolve depended on non-merge-stable CRDT repairs. Added end-to-end regression proving conflict repair remains aborted and linear after a stale conflicting merge.
Fresh-eyes follow-up: force_abort_repair created a ghost Unknown entry when called on a missing obligation ID. Missing-ID repair is now a true no-op, with a focused regression.

Fresh-eyes follow-up: Windows EnvKey updates kept stale key casing because BTreeMap::insert preserved the old equal key. env/envs/env_remove now replace equal-key entries before reinserting, and focused latest-casing regressions were added.

Fresh-eyes audit: update_from_discover consumed only delta batches and returned early on empty changes, so late-joining or shared-consumer balancers could stay permanently empty despite a live discovery snapshot. Reconciled topology against discover.endpoints() after applying deltas and added a focused late-join regression.

Fresh-eyes review of fellow-agent session-reset changes: only bootstrap operations carried worker identity, so after a worker reboot a stale higher-sequence message from the replaced worker could be accepted as fresh, poison last_inbound_seq_no, and block the new session. Added explicit worker-session identity to worker-originated envelopes, rejected mismatched inbound sessions, updated the canonical worker harness, and added focused regressions.
verify_integrity ignored schema_version tampering; hash now covers schema_version and regression added
Self-links were double-indexed in task_index, duplicating peer views and resolve_exits() actions.
poll ignored wake/next-deadline semantics; chaos delay rebased from poll time; closed-fault HUP was lost on idle polls; injected close survived clear_fault_config
Latched timer/fallback wake readiness so fired Sleep cannot be re-armed when later polled under a different earlier clock
TimerDriver::update now fails closed when asked to update a stale or cancelled handle instead of silently registering a new timer
Registration::deregister kept Drop armed on persistent explicit deregister error/panic so consumed handles still get a final best-effort cleanup pass; added direct drop-cleanup regression
DnsCache now stores negative no-record entries using negative_ttl and serves them case-insensitively until expiry instead of ignoring CacheConfig::negative_ttl.
Resolver now fail-closes no-record lookup errors into cached NoRecords results, skips pointless retries for them, and classifies NXDOMAIN-like std::net errors for negative caching.
Deep audit: atomic state machine (u64 bit-packing CAS), HalfOpen probe logic with epoch-based stale prevention, sliding window, metrics consistency, cooldown/recovery — all SOUND

Deep audit: retry budget (value-based policy chain correct), no backoff overflow (immediate retries), cancel-clean drop, idempotency delegated to Policy trait, cooperative budget prevents monopolization
Deep audit: protocol enforcement via move semantics, ObligationToken drop guard with double-panic suppression, cancel-correctness, no deadlock risk — all SOUND

Deep re-audit: double-execution prevention (WakeState + lock), waker correctness (finish_poll re-schedule), cancel propagation (inject_cancel + cancel lane), Tarjan SCC deadlock detection, EXP3 adaptive (latent f64 overflow after ~700 epochs, degrades benignly) — all SOUND

validation follow-up: panicking snapshot regression test used an if+panic pattern that tripped clippy::manual_assert and blocked repo-wide lint validation

FABRIC IR implementation follow-up: replaced placeholder with full deterministic schema types and added cut-policy validation coverage to avoid dead-code warning drift in the direct contract-test compile path

MEDIUM: Gzip/Deflate double-finish appended spurious empty compressed stream corrupting output. Fixed with finished flag. Also audited: flush deferral, chunked state machine, memory growth, identity pass-through — all SOUND.

Deep audit: timer drift (1ms worst case, correct), lost timers (skip optimization consults all bitmaps), cascade (absolute deadline re-insertion), cancellation (generation-based lazy cleanup), wrap-around (584M years) — all SOUND
Deep re-audit with execution flow tracing: acquire (FIFO + single mutex), release (cascade wake), cancel (baton passing), try_acquire (lock-only), overflow (saturating_add) — all SOUND
Deep trace: UTF-8 boundary handling, bytes_read accounting across pending chunks, CRLF normalization, cooperative yield, cancel safety — all correct
Content type matching (grpc-web vs grpc-web-text prefix distinction), trailer encoding (percent-encode grpc-message, strip CRLF in metadata) — correct

Fresh-eyes oracle audit: check() only treated Reserved as a violation, so Leaked obligations could pass region-close verification. Fixed by treating any non-success state as a leak and added a regression for ObligationState::Leaked.

Fresh-eyes oracle audit: check() iterated HashMap/HashSet state directly, making violating region selection and unrun_finalizers ordering nondeterministic. Fixed by sorting regions and finalizer ids before reporting, with regression coverage.

Fresh-eyes follow-up: oracle evaluated completed tasks at check() time instead of freezing evidence at on_region_close(), so post-close completion could mask a real structured-concurrency breach. Fixed by capturing violations at close time and added a regression.

Fresh-eyes follow-up: oracle evaluated finalizer state at check() time instead of on_region_close(), so a finalizer running after close could erase a real quiescence violation. Fixed by freezing violations at close time and added a regression.

Fresh-eyes follow-up: oracle evaluated obligation state at check() time instead of on_region_close(), so a post-close resolve could hide a real obligation-leak violation. Fixed by freezing violations at close time, keeping snapshot reconstruction deterministic, and added a regression.
Deep trace: Lyapunov potential + Azuma-Hoeffding + Freedman bounds, stall detection, convergence gating — mathematically correct
CAS + reason write-lock for hierarchical propagation, correct lock ordering, listener notification — SOUND
Product semiring combine/meet correct, identity element (INFINITE) verified, quota consumption guarded, trace logging correct
BinaryHeap min-heap ordering correct, lazy cancellation, advance_to sort is redundant but harmless, cleanup heuristic correct
ReadDir state machine (Idle/Pending/Done), spawn_blocking_io delegation, Arc<DirEntry> for metadata offload — correct

CompressionMiddleware now negotiates only encodings this build can actually serve and returns 406 when Accept-Encoding explicitly rejects every available representation; added regressions for explicit rejection and compression-disabled identity fallback

Fresh-eyes audit: whitespace-only Accept-Encoding was not normalized to empty-header semantics, so gzip-only configurations could reject a blank header with None instead of selecting the first supported encoding. Fixed by trimming before negotiation and added regression coverage.

Fresh-eyes DNS audit: lookup_ip() was stripping one trailing root dot before cache lookup/store, aliasing absolute names onto non-dotted names and risking search-domain-sensitive cache corruption. Kept dot trimming only for length validation and added regressions for dotted positive/negative cache isolation.

Fresh-eyes DNS audit follow-up: verified cache normalization stays case-insensitive without collapsing trailing-dot absolute names onto non-dotted keys, and added focused regression coverage for dotted-vs-non-dotted separation.

Peer-review fix: Fabric::connect() now shares in-process state per endpoint instead of creating isolated per-handle state; added same-endpoint share and cross-endpoint isolation regressions and isolated unit-test endpoints to avoid cross-test contamination.
Fresh-eyes diff audit: WakerState::drain_woken() depended on hash iteration order; switched to deterministic set backing plus stable sorted drain order and added regression coverage.
Fixed major handshake DoS bug. QuicEndpoint::accept now returns QuicIncoming instead of awaiting handshake directly, allowing concurrent handshakes.
Fixed RFC 9002 loss detection. Used global largest_acked rather than current ACK max for time/packet threshold.

deep CRDT audit: all JoinSemilattice impls correct. MEDIUM design note: width-mismatch infinite anti-entropy loop (filed separately)
deep audit: resolved guard correct, token lifecycle sound, all paths checked
Fresh-eyes deep trace of WaiterSlab scan_start hint maintenance. Correctly tracks the lowest index of any unnotified active waiter. O(1) amortized guarantees hold.
Fresh-eyes: Two-phase commit explicit abort Drop logic is fully sound.
Validated fail-closed LocalTaskStore cross-generation slot reuse rejection plus focused regression under asupersync-1qfd0.388
Fixed critical data-loss bug in ORSet. Removals were not leaving tombstones, causing subsequent merges from older replicas to silently undo the removal.
Fresh-eyes deep trace of unix socket reactor registration. Correctly tracks registration state and correctly avoids blocking.
Fresh-eyes deep trace of connection manager and push_count bounds. Correct.
Fresh-eyes diff audit: TaskInspector::render_summary ignored highlight_stuck_tasks and always emitted the stuck-task section; fixed config-gated rendering and added focused regression tests.
MEDIUM: LagSketch/AdvisoryAggregate width mismatch infinite anti-entropy loop (empty adopts, non-empty breaks loop). LOW: InterestSummary unsubscribe on absent pattern created unbounded zero entries.

Deep audit: positive sub-millisecond TTLs truncated to zero and produced invalid/unsafe Redis expiry behavior; centralized TTL encoding now rejects zero and rounds positive durations up to 1ms for SET PX / PEXPIRE.

Peer-review diff audit under asupersync-28c51.302: fixed manual FederationBridge PartialEq ignoring runtime state, then corrected the follow-on normalization attempt by aligning equality with the configured-bridge contract and ignoring ephemeral runtime entirely so serde round-trips preserve equality, and made plan_replication_catch_up fail closed when remote_sequence exceeds local_sequence instead of reporting AlreadyConverged.

Peer-review diff audit: SparseRow::singleton accepted out-of-range indices when the coefficient was zero because it returned zeros(len) before validating bounds; moved the bounds check ahead of the zero fast path and added a focused regression.

Peer-review audit: public EncodingStats leaked an internal usize::MAX sentinel as degree_min before any repairs were emitted; initialized the degree fields to zero, seeded degree_min from the first repair sample, and added a regression for the zero-repair state.

Exploratory audit through driver/wheel/sleep found stale timer-wheel time on register/update/query paths: the driver measured new deadlines against a wheel tick that only advanced during expiry collection, so long idle gaps could clamp short future timers into the past and fire them immediately. Driver paths now synchronize the wheel to the current clock before register/update/next_deadline, with focused idle-gap regressions.

Exploratory audit through driver/wheel/sleep found stale internal current_tick state leaking into register/query behavior. Added a crate-visible synchronize(now) helper so non-expiry callers can advance buckets/overflow promotion to the current clock without draining ready timers; collect_expired now reuses the same path.
Exploratory diff audit as timer-path caller while tracing idle-gap behavior; no additional sleep-state bug confirmed after re-reading timer registration/update/wake flow against the driver fix.

Fresh-eyes follow-up on the timer idle-gap fix: register/update/next_deadline were still sampling clock.now() before contending on the wheel mutex, so a long lock wait plus clock advance could preserve a smaller stale-time misclassification. Moved the time sample under the wheel lock and added a lock-contention regression for absolute-deadline registration.

Exploratory archaeology through spork/app/supervision/runtime found unified SporkError severity flattening transient region-capacity backpressure into permanent start/spawn failures; severity now preserves nested RegionCreateError::ParentAtCapacity and SpawnError::RegionAtCapacity semantics, with focused regressions.

Peer review of weighted multicast/quorum routing found select_n_weighted_round_robin advancing rr_counter by requested picks instead of actual weighted-ring slots consumed while skipping duplicates, which restarted later calls inside the same heavy block and starved lighter endpoints. Fixed counter advancement with a CAS loop over consumed slots and added cross-call progression regressions.

Exploratory DNS archaeology found hostname validation was only length-based and ran too late. lookup_ip/do_lookup_ip now fail-close invalid empty-label and >63-byte-label hostnames before cache or resolver fallback, with regressions covering cache-before-validation, empty labels, and overlong labels.

Peer review of a recent half-open sliding-window follow-up found the close-time reset was wrong because Open transitions already clear the window; removing it preserves successful probe history and prevents overly aggressive reopen after recovery. Also replaced the new regression with a compile-correct test using the real record_failure/record_success signatures.

Peer review of JoinHandle found the new executor-side disappearance panic path was terminal in poll() but not reflected in is_finished(), so dropped-task handles could be misreported as still live. Fixed is_finished() to recognize the strong-count terminal state and added a regression covering the dropped-task panic contract.

Fresh-eyes reread of the weighted multicast/quorum selector found select_n_weighted_round_robin still stepping one weighted slot at a time while skipping duplicates, making extreme weight skew effectively O(total_weight). Rewrote it to jump directly to the next endpoint block boundary while preserving rr_counter semantics and added an extreme-skew regression.

Deep obligation audit found two concrete correctness issues. First, single-symbol obligations accepted progress for the wrong SymbolId or via bulk fulfill_many without symbol identity; fulfillment now validates the exact expected symbol/object and rejects bulk progress on single-symbol obligations. Second, terminal obligations only updated by_id state and never deregistered from object/task/region secondary indexes, so active-obligation queries returned dead IDs and the registry grew unbounded over time; the obligation now carries a full registry mirror and unregisters itself from every index on terminal resolution, with focused regressions for active-only lookup behavior.

Random process-lifecycle audit found `Child::wait()` and `wait_async()` no longer matched std semantics after spawn extracted `ChildStdin` into the wrapper: both wait paths left the parent write end open, so children waiting for stdin EOF could hang forever while the caller waited for exit. Both wait paths now close `self.stdin` first, with focused sync/async EOF regressions. Local rustfmt/diff checks and `rch cargo check -p asupersync --lib` are green; the focused lib-test selector was blocked by an unrelated current-tree `src/raptorq/gf256.rs` compile error (`Gf256Kernel::X86Avx2` missing at line 4310).

Deep audit found the G7 action chooser delegating to generic `LossMatrix::bayes_action()`, which breaks equal expected-loss ties by lowest index even though the contract/docs/artifact require the conservative deterministic order `fallback > rollback > canary_hold > continue`. Replaced `choose_action()` with a contract-specific comparator that preserves that safety ordering and added `choose_action_uses_conservative_tie_breaker` covering continue/canary_hold and rollback/fallback ties. Validation: `git diff --check -- src/raptorq/decision_contract.rs` and `rustfmt +nightly --edition 2024 --check src/raptorq/decision_contract.rs` passed locally; `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-target-amber-dc-check cargo check -p asupersync --lib` passed remotely; `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-target-amber-dc-test2 cargo test -p asupersync --lib choose_action_uses_conservative_tie_breaker -- --nocapture` passed remotely; focused `rch` clippy still fails only on unrelated existing files (`src/lab/dual_run.rs`, `src/net/tcp/stream.rs`, `src/record/symbolic_obligation.rs`, `src/spork.rs`, `src/transport/router.rs`).

Peer-review follow-up on the fresh spectral/trapped-cycle changes found the live governor guard had been widened from `wait_graph_nodes > 1` to `> 0`, which started recording spectral history for ordinary singleton live-task states because `wait_graph_signals_from_state()` counts every non-terminal task, not just tasks participating in nontrivial wait topology. Narrowed the guard to `trapped_wait_cycle || wait_graph_nodes > 1` so trapped self-cycles still feed the monitor while benign singleton graphs stay out of the live spectral/decision-contract path, and added `test_governor_single_live_task_without_wait_edges_skips_spectral_monitor` alongside the trapped-self-cycle regression. Validation: `git diff --check -- src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs` and `rustfmt +nightly --edition 2024 --check src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs` passed locally; `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-target-amber-spectral-check cargo check -p asupersync --lib` passed remotely; `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-target-amber-spectral-test cargo test -p asupersync --lib test_governor_single_ -- --nocapture` passed remotely; focused `rch` clippy still fails only on unrelated existing files (`src/lab/dual_run.rs`, `src/net/tcp/stream.rs`, `src/record/symbolic_obligation.rs`, `src/spork.rs`, `src/transport/router.rs`).

Fresh-eyes audit found two concrete correctness issues. First, TaskInspector measured task age and snapshot time from timer_driver().now() even though task timestamps are recorded against RuntimeState::now, so runtimes without a timer driver collapsed stuck-task age to zero; the inspector now uses the runtime logical clock with a regression covering age, stuck_count, and generated_at. Second, inspect_task() reported every obligation whose holder matched the task, including committed entries still retained in the table; it now uses the pending-holder index so only live obligations are reported, with a focused regression for committed-vs-pending visibility.

Fresh-eyes follow-up on recent half-open sliding-window changes found two remaining issues: a stale regression still encoded the wrong model (it assumed close-time reset, rather than open-time reset, was responsible for clearing stale failures), and the live HalfOpen->Closed success path still performed that close-time sliding-window reset. The regression now asserts the real invariant and the runtime path preserves successful probe history after recovery.

Deep audit of Redis pubsub flow found a real reliability bug: RedisPubSub::ping() consumed and discarded interleaved Message/Subscription events while waiting for PONG, so liveness probes could lose real pubsub traffic. Fixed by adding a pending_events queue, routing next_event() through it, and buffering ping interleavings instead of dropping them; added a focused socket-level regression.

Fresh-eyes follow-up on the earlier TaskInspector audit found the clock-source fix had over-corrected toward `RuntimeState::now`. Live runtimes advance observability time through the timer driver while `state.now` is primarily maintained in timerless/lab paths, so always reading `state.now` could underreport task age and snapshot time. `current_time()` now prefers `timer_driver().now()` when available and falls back to `state.now` only when no timer driver exists, with a mixed-mode regression proving timer-driver preference and timerless fallback.

Fresh-eyes diff audit of the active explorer surface found two concrete queue-management bugs. First, DporExplorer and TopologyExplorer only deduplicated against explored seeds, so the same derived seed could be queued multiple times before first execution; both now track pending seeds to keep the work queue/frontier unique. Second, both explore loops popped one extra seed before enforcing max_runs, which silently dropped the next unexplored seed from report.top_unexplored; the run-budget guard now fires before popping, with focused regressions for both explorers.

Random deep archaeology through service load balancing and discovery found constructor-time topology reconciliation drift in the weighted strategy. First, LoadBalancer::empty() never called Strategy::sync_backend_count, so strategies with pre-seeded topology metadata started out inconsistent with an actually empty backend set. Second, Weighted::sync_backend_count only grew vectors instead of reconciling them exactly to the live backend count, leaving phantom weight/current_weight slots after constructor initialization with fewer backends than weights. Empty/new now force exact alignment, with focused regressions for empty weighted balancers and shrinking pre-seeded weighted state to the actual backend count.

Peer-review audit of the recent pubsub ping buffering change found a reconnect isolation bug: ping() now preserves interleaved events in pending_events, but reconnect() replaced the underlying connection and restored subscriptions without clearing that buffer, so next_event() could return stale pre-reconnect messages from the dead session before reading the new socket. reconnect() now drops buffered events when swapping sessions, with a focused regression proving reconnect only exposes fresh post-reconnect traffic.

Peer-review of the DPOR explorer path found the sleep-set deduplication key was too coarse: SleepSet::bp_key() hashed only divergence/index shape plus event-kind discriminants, so two different races with the same structural shape could alias and cause unsound cross-trace pruning. Fixed by hashing the semantic trace_event_key() of both race endpoints instead, and added a regression proving same-shape races on different tasks no longer collide.

Fresh-eyes follow-up on the new sleep-set semantic-key hardening found the backtrack-point key was still directional. After exploring one ordering of a dependent race, the same race encountered in reverse endpoint order produced a different key and could be redundantly re-enqueued. SleepSet::bp_key() now normalizes the two semantic endpoint keys before hashing, with a regression proving reverse-order traces deduplicate while different-task same-shape races remain distinct.

Peer-review of the recent kill_on_drop reaper change found the runtime-thread detection heuristic was too narrow. should_background_reap_kill_on_drop() only checked Cx::current(), but runtime worker threads keep Runtime::current_handle() installed even when no task is actively being polled, so dropping a Child on a worker outside task-poll context could still take the blocking wait path and stall the worker. The guard now treats either an ambient Cx or an installed runtime handle as reason to offload the reap, with a regression covering worker-thread startup before the first task poll.

Peer-review of the recent weighted-topology reconciliation change found a configuration-loss regression: `LoadBalancer::empty()` now called `sync_backend_count(0)` and `Weighted::sync_backend_count()` shrank the weights vector to the live backend count, so caller-supplied deferred weights were silently discarded for empty or partially materialized balancers. `Weighted` now tracks live backend count separately from the configured weight plan, preserving deferred weights for later insertions while keeping live SWRR state aligned with actual backends; added focused regressions for empty and partial-constructor activation.
LOW: prune_expired did not prune expired default routes. Also verified weighted RR select_n, TTL enforcement, and dispatch guard
